stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers and llama.
The primary use case demonstrated is text-generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: Google's SynthID AI watermarking tech is being adopted by OpenAI, Nvidia, and more
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google's SynthID watermarking technology is gaining traction across the AI industry.
Major players like OpenAI and Nvidia are adopting this technology.
The adoption aims to combat the proliferation of AI-generated misinformation and distinguish truth from fiction.

stories.title: In stunning display of stupid, secret CISA credentials found in public GitHub repo
stories.author: Lee Hutchinson
bullet_points: Sensitive credentials (SSH keys, plaintext passwords) were exposed in a public GitHub repository.
The exposed data included CISA credentials, indicating a high-stakes security breach.
The sensitive information was publicly accessible since November 2025.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash might be fast enough for gen AI to make sense
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Gemini 3.5 Flash is highlighted by Google as a key component for future agentic AI.
The model's primary advantage is its increased efficiency, suggesting faster deployment and lower operational costs.
The news points toward a shift in AI development focusing on practical, fast, and scalable AI agents.

stories.title: Toto 2.0: Time Series Forecasting Enters the Scaling Era
stories.author: David Asker
bullet_points: Introduces Toto 2.0, a family of open-weights time series foundation models demonstrating scalability.
The models show reliable forecast-quality improvements across a wide parameter range (4M to 2.5B) using a single training recipe.
Toto 2.0 sets new state-of-the-art results on major forecasting benchmarks, including GIFT-Eval and TIME.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash
stories.author: spectraldrift
bullet_points: Google has released Gemini 3.5 Flash, indicating a new iteration in their AI model lineup.
The release is documented via the official Google AI developer API documentation.
The naming convention suggests a focus on speed and efficiency ('Flash') within the Gemini family.

stories.title: Show HN: Forge – Guardrails take an 8B model from 53% to 99% on agentic tasks
stories.author: zambelli
bullet_points: Introduces 'Forge,' an open-source reliability layer for self-hosted LLM tool-calling.
The system significantly boosts the performance of local 8B models from 53% to 99% on complex agentic tasks.
Forge achieves this improvement by adding domain-agnostic guardrails (e.g., error recovery, step enforcement) without modifying the base model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes common ML ecosystem components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating its primary application in generative AI.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Apple devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local, on-device operation.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own hardware for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files for setup.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis framed current AI progress as potentially being the 'foothills of the singularity.'
Hassabis emphasized that Google's research aims to unlock the potential of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) for global benefit.
The statement was delivered during the Google I/O keynote, signaling high-level corporate focus on advanced AI development.

stories.title: The future of Google is a search box that does everything
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's strategic shift is moving beyond simple search functionality.
The focus is on creating an all-in-one assistant experience accessible via the search box.
This represents a significant evolution in how Google intends to interact with users.

stories.title: TIDE: Efficient and Lossless MoE Diffusion LLM Inference with I/O-aware Expert Offload
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Introduces TIDE, a method for efficient and lossless inference of MoE Diffusion LLMs (dLLMs).
Addresses the deployment challenge of large-scale dLLMs on resource-constrained devices.
Combats existing bottlenecks by implementing I/O-aware expert offloading to improve hardware utilization.

stories.title: ClinSeekAgent: Automating Multimodal Evidence Seeking for Agentic Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces ClinSeekAgent, a framework designed to automate dynamic, multimodal evidence seeking for clinical reasoning.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM applications in medicine: the assumption that evidence is pre-curated.
Shifts the paradigm from passive information consumption to active, iterative planning and synthesis of heterogeneous clinical data.

stories.title: ThoughtTrace: Understanding User Thoughts in Real-World LLM Interactions
stories.author: Tianmin Shu
bullet_points: Introduces ThoughtTrace, a novel, large-scale dataset designed to capture users' internal thoughts during LLM interactions, moving beyond just transcribed dialogue.
The dataset pairs real-world multi-turn conversations with self-reported user thoughts, including prompt motivations and reactions to AI responses.
ThoughtTrace was collected across 20 different language models and involves over 1,000 users, providing a rich resource for understanding user intent in conversational AI.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash Developer Guide
stories.author: pat_loeber
bullet_points: Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available (GA).
The model is reported to be stable and production-ready.
The announcement targets developers for scaled implementation.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely offline.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model serving and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Mercedes’ electric AMG GT 4-door coupe can go 0-60 in 2 seconds
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Mercedes has unveiled its new electric AMG GT 4-door coupe, signaling a push into ultra-high performance EVs.
The vehicle boasts impressive performance, capable of reaching 0-60 mph in just 2 seconds.
The design and technology draw inspiration from Mercedes' previous XX concept, known for its endurance testing.

stories.title: Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google is heavily promoting its AI-powered future, emphasizing the need for user trust.
New tools like Gemini Spark (an always-on AI agent) and Daily Brief were showcased at I/O 2026.
These new features aim to integrate AI into daily life to assist with tasks like event organization.

stories.title: FBI seeks US-wide access to license plate cameras, wants "data in near real time"
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FBI is seeking nationwide access to license plate camera data.
The request involves paying vendors to facilitate tracking and searching of vehicles across the US.
A key requirement is the ability to access this data in 'near real time'.

stories.title: "I'll buy 10 of those"—NASA science chief yearns for mass-produced satellites
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's science chief is advocating for the mass production of satellites to increase scientific data collection in space.
The core challenge identified is the current limitation in deploying a sufficient volume of scientific instruments.
This points toward a potential industry shift toward more scalable and commercially viable space infrastructure.

stories.title: Two AI-based science assistants succeed with drug-retargeting tasks
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Two AI-based science assistants were developed to assist with drug-retargeting tasks.
The tools are capable of generating scientific hypotheses.
One of the assistants further analyzes the data generated from the hypotheses.

stories.title: A Methodology for Selecting and Composing Runtime Architecture Patterns for Production LLM Agents
stories.author: Vasundra Srinivasan
bullet_points: The paper introduces the 'stochastic-deterministic boundary' (SDB) as a critical architectural concept for production LLM agents.
The SDB defines a formal, four-part contract (proposer, verifier, commit, reject) governing how LLM outputs translate into system actions.
It posits that the SDB is the fundamental, load-bearing primitive for building robust, production-grade AI agents.

stories.title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
stories.author: Md. Zakir Hossen
bullet_points: Introduces HaorFloodAlert, a novel ML ensemble designed for 72-hour flood prediction in Bangladesh's Haor wetlands.
Addresses a critical gap in current flood modeling, which fails to account for the unique backwater dynamics of flat basins, unlike riverine flood models.
The model successfully mitigates the impact of seasonality (e.g., temperature bias) on prediction accuracy.

stories.title: Not Every Rubric Teaches Equally: Policy-Aware Rubric Rewards for RLVR
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in rubric-based rewards for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Standard aggregation methods fail to distinguish between a criterion's inherent human importance and its current utility as an optimization signal.
The paper proposes a 'policy-aware' rubric reward mechanism to better guide model training based on qualitative criteria.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: The study addresses the critical issue of visual attribution in Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) used for medical imaging (Chest X-rays).
A key concern is that current visual explanations may not accurately reflect the actual visual evidence driving the model's clinical decisions, impacting trustworthiness.
The research tackles the challenge of verifying model reasoning when ground-truth annotations for internal model processes are unavailable.

stories.title: Less Back-and-Forth: A Comparative Study of Structured Prompting
stories.author: Abdou Sow
bullet_points: The study investigates how structured prompting techniques (checklist vs. clarifying questions) can improve LLM output quality.
It compares three prompt conditions against four diverse task types: summarization, planning, explanation, and coding.
The core goal is to reduce the 'back-and-forth' interaction required when using underspecified prompts with LLMs.

stories.title: From Seeing to Thinking: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning Improves Post-Training of Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: The study investigates the performance bottleneck in Vision-Language Models (VLMs), suggesting visual perception, not reasoning, is the primary limitation.
The research proposes decoupling VLM capabilities into three distinct training stages: visual perception, visual reasoning, and textual reasoning.
This modular approach aims to improve VLM post-training by specializing the training for each component.

stories.title: KoRe: Compact Knowledge Representations for Large Language Models
stories.author: Jacopo Staiano
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent flaw in LLMs encoding world knowledge solely within opaque parameters.
It proposes 'KoRe,' a method for creating compact knowledge representations to improve LLM knowledge handling.
The work contrasts LLM parameter-based knowledge with the human-readable, structured nature of Knowledge Graphs.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) are being explored for medical applications like Chest X-ray reasoning.
A key concern is the lack of trustworthy visual grounding in LVLM predictions, which is critical for clinical use.
The paper addresses the verification gap in existing visual attribution methods used to explain model decisions.

stories.title: MixRea: Benchmarking Explicit-Implicit Reasoning in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yanru Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MixRea, a new benchmark for evaluating explicit-implicit reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Investigates if LLMs suffer from 'inattentional blindness,' failing to notice subtle cues despite explicit instructions.
Addresses the growing concern over LLM reliability in high-stakes decision-making contexts.

stories.title: BalanceRAG: Joint Risk Calibration for Cascaded Retrieval-Augmented Generation
stories.author: Zhiyuan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces 'BalanceRAG,' a method for optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by using a cascaded approach.
The system first uses an LLM-only branch and only escalates to RAG if the initial model is uncertain.
A key contribution is the joint risk calibration across the cascade stages to improve overall utility and trustworthiness.

stories.title: CopT: Contrastive On-Policy Thinking with Continuous Spaces for General and Agentic Reasoning
stories.author: Wenke Lee
bullet_points: Introduces CopT, a new reasoning pipeline designed to improve upon standard Chain-of-Thought (CoT) methods in LLMs.
Addresses the inefficiency of CoT by allowing for 'performative reasoning,' where models can answer before extensive thinking is completed.
CopT utilizes a contrastive, on-policy approach with continuous spaces to enhance general and agentic reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Google changes its search box
stories.author: berkeleyjunk
bullet_points: Google is reportedly integrating AI features, specifically Gemini, into its search bar.
The change represents a significant evolution in how Google delivers search results, moving towards generative AI answers.
The news originates from Hacker News and is corroborated by links to The New York Times and TechCrunch.

stories.title: AI isn't replacing junior devs. Your org chart is.
stories.author: dennistraub
bullet_points: The article reframes the debate around AI replacing junior developers by distinguishing between individual capability and organizational structure.
The core argument suggests that the true danger lies not in AI itself, but in how organizations structure their development pipelines.
It warns that poor pipeline decision-making, potentially influenced by AI discussions, poses a significant risk to development teams.

stories.title: Google AI Edge Gallery Now Runs MCP On-Device. The Privacy Architecture
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: Google AI Edge Gallery now supports running MCP (likely a specific model/framework) directly on-device.
The article focuses on the privacy architecture enabling this on-device AI capability.
The content is presented as a submission for the Google I/O Writing Challenge, suggesting a technical deep dive.

stories.title: Microsoft Just Published a Blueprint for Self-Healing CI/CD. Here's What the Observe-Analyze-Act Loop Actually Does.
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: Microsoft has released a blueprint detailing a self-healing Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) system.
The core concept revolves around an 'Observe-Analyze-Act' feedback loop to automate pipeline recovery.
This addresses the common engineering friction caused by recurring pipeline failures.
